compiled_state.title: Route all config reads through Config; retire both inline parsers; preserve fail-closed gate
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What
Collapse the two ad-hoc inline "="-splitting parsers into the central Config: src/rebar/_engine_support/lookups.py::_display_mode (ticket.display_mode) and src/rebar/_commands/txn.py verify-gate loop (verify.*).

## CRITICAL
Preserve the fail-closed verify gate (txn.py:239-270): present-but-unreadable verify.* => require signature; absent => gate off.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both inline parsers removed; reads go through Config
- [ ] Regression test: present-but-unparseable verify config => require_sig=True; absent => gate off
- [ ] Full non-integration suite green
Blocked by the typed-Config task.

comments.body: DONE: routed both inline config parsers through the unified load_config. (1) txn.py verify gate: require_sig = load_config(cfg_root).verify.require_signature_for_close, except ConfigError => require_sig=True (FAIL-CLOSED preserved + extended — the gate can now be set from ANY layer: rebar.toml/pyproject/env/legacy). (2) lookups.py _display_mode: load_config(...).ticket.display_mode, fail-OPEN to 'auto' (display non-critical); the DSO WORKFLOW_CONFIG_FILE read is dropped as a side effect. (3) Test isolation: new autouse _isolate_user_config in tests/conftest.py points XDG_CONFIG_HOME at an empty per-test dir + drops REBAR_CONFIG so no test reads the dev's real ~/.config/rebar/config.toml (portability). Subagent review found a REAL fail-OPEN security gap (a malformed pyproject carrying [tool.rebar] was silently skipped in discovery -> gate off) — FIXED: _pyproject_rebar_state returns has/absent/unreadable, and an 'unreadable' pyproject is selected as the would-be config so _read_toml_table raises ConfigError -> fail-closed (scoped behind rebar.toml precedence; 'absent' pyproject still skipped, doesn't preempt legacy). Added regression tests (loader + gate level). Full offline suite green (1877 passed). Perf no-caching deferred to e211.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
